description:
# Kill best_fn_types' multi-spec bail; per-spec fold inside codegen

## The smell

`ir_fold::best_fn_types` (lines 28-39) returns:
- the any-key spec, OR
- the single narrow spec when there's exactly one, OR
- `None` (bails) when there are multiple narrow specs.

For functions specialized at multiple callsites (e.g. ast_eval's `eval`
with arity-2 vs arity-3 specialization), `best_fn_types` returns None
and **no folding happens**. The infra is there; the per-spec wiring
isn't. Compounded with .43.A this is the wasted leverage.

## What changes

In codegen's per-spec emission loop, before lowering a spec to CLIF:
- clone the source `FnIr` for this spec
- run `fold_fn` on the clone, passing this spec's `FnTypes` directly
- lower the folded clone

The pre-codegen `fold_module` stays — keeps single-spec fns folded
in-place, no behavior change for them.

## What stays

- `fold_module` (the pre-codegen pass) — folds single-spec/any-key fns,
  unchanged behavior for them.
- `best_fn_types` — its callers will narrow over time, but it's still
  the right fallback for the global pre-codegen pass.

## What's removed

- The multi-spec-bail. When .43.A's narrowing lands per-spec, the dead
  TypeTest arms in ast_eval's specialized specs fold to constants;
  ir_dce removes the dead branches.

## Acceptance

- ast_eval's per-spec CLIF strictly smaller for specialized specs
  (verify before/after).
- All other fixtures: golden byte-identical OR strictly smaller.
  Re-bless honestly; review every diff.
- Three-path parity holds (interp/JIT/AOT all still pass).

## Risk

Cloning IR per spec uses memory only when >1 spec exists (minority of
fns). Acceptable.

The fold pass mutates terminators (If → Goto). Running it twice on the
same FnIr (once pre-codegen with any-key, once per-spec) means the
per-spec pass sees a partially-folded IR. Should be safe — fold is
monotonic. Tests confirm.

## Why second

Step .43.A turns on the information; .43.B turns on the consumption.
The two compound and prove the typer's per-callsite spec investment
already pays off — before any matrix work.
title: fz-ul4.43.B: ir_fold runs per-spec
